summary: IDF eliminated multiple Hamas and Hezbollah operatives in Gaza and southern Lebanon, while security forces detained a suspected Hamas infiltrator near Karm Shalom. Hezbollah gunmen were targeted near the Ali Taher ridge in southern Lebanon. Ongoing tensions include civilian casualties in Gaza and heightened protests in Israel.
key_points: IDF killed Hamas operative Akram Mahmad Abu Matsi after he entered Israel during the Oct 7 attack (Source: @pkpoi/142958).
IDF forces detained an unarmed Hamas suspect near the Karm Shalom crossing and transferred him for interrogation (Source: @amitsegal/62499).
Two Hezbollah gunmen were struck by IDF air and ground forces near the Ali Taher ridge area in southern Lebanon (Source: @manniefabian/58670).
IDF air force engaged two armed Hezbollah suspects crossing the security zone on the Ali Taher ridge, eliminating the threat (Source: @idf_telegram/24434).
A contractor worker was killed by a building collapse in the Gaza Strip (Source: @almogboker78/17581).
Hezbollah drone attacks caused injuries to Israeli soldiers in southern Lebanon (Source: @lelotsenzura/93197).
US Secretary of State Marco Rubio indicated technical talks with Iran will continue until June 30 (Source: @N12chat/214516).
Iranian officials discussed ongoing negotiations over the nuclear file and sanctions with US technical teams (Source: @naya_foriraq/79809).

sections.executive_summary: IDF eliminated a Hamas operative linked to the Oct 7 attack and detained another suspect near Karm Shalom. In southern Lebanon, Israeli forces struck two Hezbollah gunmen near the Ali Taher ridge, following drone‑reported engagements. Parallel developments include civilian casualties in Gaza and diplomatic talks between the US and Iran.

points: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio said technical negotiations with Iran will continue until June 30, involving technical teams (Source: @N12chat/214516).
Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi spoke with a senior Hamas official about ongoing aggression against Gaza and related international forums (Source: @asafroz/50680).
US Treasury announced a monitoring office in Doha to oversee the use of released Iranian assets for food and medical imports (Source: @VahidOnline/76652).

Iranian officials claimed that US sanctions relief would not include tolls or insurance costs for Iranian shipping, and the US would release some funds for agricultural purchases (Source: @asafroz/50688).
Iranian officials asserted that the Strait of Hormuz would no longer be blockable by Iran, noting mines have delayed oil flow normalization (Source: @naya_foriraq/79814).
IDF spokesperson reported the killing of Hamas operative Akram Mahmad Abu Matsi, who entered Israel during the Oct 7 massacre (Source: @pkpoi/142958).
IDF and Shin Bet forces eliminated Hamas operative Mahmad Zaid in the Yaamon area of the Judea and Samaria Division during a joint operation (Source: @idf_telegram/24433).
IDF forces detained an unarmed Hamas suspect near the Karm Shalom crossing, confirming no breach of Israeli territory (Source: @amitsegal/62499).
Two Hezbollah gunmen were identified and struck by IDF air and ground units near the Ali Taher ridge, neutralizing the threat to Israeli troops (Source: @manniefabian/58670).
IDF air force engaged two armed Hezbollah suspects crossing the security zone on the Ali Taher ridge, destroying the vehicle and eliminating the threat (Source: @idf_telegram/24434).
Hezbollah drone attacks injured an Israeli soldier in southern Lebanon (Source: @lelotsenzura/93197).
Israel's military minister stated Israel will not withdraw forces from southern Lebanon even if requested by the US (Source: @presstv/195464).
Israeli officials warned that Hezbollah remains a direct threat to civilian populations and will be continuously targeted (Source: @almogboker78/17579).
A contractor worker was killed when a building collapsed in the Gaza Strip (Source: @almogboker78/17581).
Reports indicated Hamas is establishing new rocket launch positions in Gaza and preparing for a future campaign against Israel (Source: @almogboker78/17579).
Hezbollah documented drone strikes causing Israeli casualties in southern Lebanon, reflecting cross‑border escalation affecting Gaza‑West Bank dynamics (Source: @lelotsenzura/93197).
Israeli forces continue operations in the Judea and Samaria Division, targeting arms traffickers linked to Hamas (Source: @idf_telegram/24433).
Civilian protests in Israel over Haredi vehicle convoys are intensifying, potentially impacting security deployments in the West Bank (Source: @amitsegal/62501).
Two Hezbollah gunmen were targeted and killed by IDF forces near the Ali Taher ridge area close to Nabatieh (Source: @manniefabian/58670).
IDF air force struck two armed Hezbollah suspects crossing the security zone on the Ali Taher ridge, eliminating the threat (Source: @idf_telegram/24434).
Hezbollah drone attacks resulted in injuries to Israeli soldiers in southern Lebanon (Source: @lelotsenzura/93197).
A medium‑range rocket was reportedly fired from Lebanon toward Israeli territory, prompting air defense alerts (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/372676).
Israeli military statements emphasized continued presence in southern Lebanon despite US pressure (Source: @presstv/195464).
Iraq's Prime Minister Ali Fadel Al‑Zaydi claimed that after US forces withdraw, no justification for resistance will remain in Iraq (Source: @SharghDaily/167940).
US officials indicated technical talks with Iran will continue, potentially affecting regional dynamics in Syria and Iraq (Source: @N12chat/214516).
Iranian officials discussed ongoing aggression against Gaza in international forums, which may influence Syrian and Iraqi diplomatic positions (Source: @asafroz/50680).
US Treasury announced oversight of Iranian assets from a Doha office, signaling financial pressure on Iran that could impact its regional activities (Source: @VahidOnline/76652).
Iranian officials asserted that US sanctions relief would not include tolls or insurance costs, affecting Iranian shipping routes through the Strait of Hormuz (Source: @asafroz/50688).
US Treasury will monitor released Iranian assets from a Doha office, indicating Gulf involvement in overseeing Iranian financial flows (Source: @VahidOnline/76652).
Iranian claims that US sanctions relief excludes tolls and insurance costs for Iranian vessels, highlighting Gulf maritime concerns (Source: @asafroz/50688).
US Secretary of State Marco Rubio discussed ongoing technical negotiations with Iran, relevant to Gulf regional security (Source: @N12chat/214516).
Iranian officials noted that mines in the Strait of Hormuz have delayed oil flow normalization, affecting Gulf oil markets (Source: @naya_foriraq/79814).
US officials warned that if Iran fails to honor commitments in Switzerland, the President has many options, implying potential Gulf security repercussions (Source: @VahidOnline/76657).

key_developments: IDF eliminated Hamas operative Akram Mahmad Abu Matsi, linked to the Oct 7 attack, in Gaza (Source: @pkpoi/142958).
Two Hezbollah gunmen were killed by IDF air and ground forces near the Ali Taher ridge in southern Lebanon (Source: @manniefabian/58670).
IDF and Shin Bet neutralized Hamas arms trafficker Mahmad Zaid in the Yaamon area of Judea and Samaria (Source: @idf_telegram/24433).
A contractor worker died from a building collapse in the Gaza Strip, highlighting civilian risk (Source: @almogboker78/17581).
US Secretary of State Marco Rubio confirmed technical talks with Iran will continue until June 30 (Source: @N12chat/214516).
Hezbollah drone attacks injured Israeli soldiers in southern Lebanon, indicating ongoing cross‑border hostilities (Source: @lelotsenzura/93197).
Iraq's Prime Minister stated that post‑US withdrawal, no justification for resistance will exist in Iraq (Source: @SharghDaily/167940).
sections.threat_assessment: Hezbollah remains an active threat in southern Lebanon, demonstrated by recent armed incursions and drone attacks on Israeli forces. Hamas continues to operate within Gaza, with ongoing attempts to rebuild capabilities and launch new rocket positions. Iranian influence persists through diplomatic negotiations and financial mechanisms, potentially affecting regional stability via the Strait of Hormuz and proxy activities.
sections.regional_response: The United States is maintaining diplomatic pressure on Iran, extending technical talks and monitoring released assets from Doha, while signaling readiness to enforce sanctions if commitments lapse. Iraq's leadership signals a shift toward economic partnership with the US post‑withdrawal, reducing internal resistance narratives. Gulf states are closely monitoring Iranian maritime activities and financial flows, preparing for possible disruptions in oil markets.
sections.intelligence_notes: Future IDF operations are likely to focus on dismantling Hamas logistics networks in the West Bank and Gaza, as indicated by recent arrests and killings. Hezbollah's pattern of cross‑border attacks suggests continued Israeli air and ground strikes in the Ali Taher ridge area. Monitoring US‑Iran technical negotiations will be critical for anticipating shifts in regional proxy dynamics and sanction regimes.
